title.sub: The page was not found. The motive is unclear.

cookies.agreement1: By using this website you agree to the use of your browser's localStorage to save settings and whether you hate this message. If you do, click on the checkmark to never see it again.

language.description: Change the language used on the website. If no translation is available, English will be used instead.
language.title: Language

theme.description: Choose a color palette where blue and yellow are the main accent colors to better account for colorblindness.
theme.title: Theme

button.discordTagCopied: Magic! My Discord tag was copied to your clipboard. Now send me a friend request to write in DMs.

contact.text: If your question wasn't answered here, or you just want to text to me directly, do so with one of these:
contact.title: Still no?

1commission.long: Generally speaking, I don't work for others, unless it's for people I know well enough for me to consider them so-called friends. There are several reasons why I won't work for strangers for the time being, one of which is the lack of a proper payment method that I could accept.
1commission.title: Can you make a (thing here) for me?

downloads.text1: As the name might suggest, I encourage you to download and use things on the
downloads.text2: for yourself. You are also free to make your own changes and share them privately with your friends. However, I do not allow you to resell or otherwise redistribute my projects without my permission.
downloads.title: Fonts, wallpapers and other downloads.

fonts.text1: My fonts are licensed under the
fonts.text2: . This means that you may freely use the fonts for personal and commercial projects. However, you may not sell the fonts and/or derivatives thereof.
fonts.title: Fonts

2terms.long: There are some exceptions to this as mentioned below. If you're ever not sure about what you may do with a thing I made, please don't hesitate to get in touch with me and ask me.
2terms.reminder: If these terms are unclear to you in any way, don't hesitate to get in touch with me.
2terms.title: What are the terms of use for your creations?
2terms.tldr: Generally, you are not allowed to re-use, sell or otherwise redistribute my works in any way unless stated otherwise.

works.text2: I'd rather you simply focus your senses on them and just look at them. Think of that and my YouTube channel as my little art gallery where you can have a look at my works. No buy, no rent, no nothing. Only look.
works.title: Animations, illustrations and other works.

4gamecube.long:
since I had only used a Wii U in recent years. Make of that what you will.
If you don't know what this question is about, have a look at the comments below
4gamecube.title: Why did you forget the GameCube disc?
4gamecube.tldr: I forgot the original Wii had that backwards compatibility,

5support.long: Also feel free to give me some feedback to help me improve. If you think your friends might like what I make as well, tell them about me. If you want to help me afford licenses needed to make my stuff, have a look at my fancy
5support.title: How can I support you?
5support.tldr: Kind words go a long way.

6actually.text: Nah, just every once in a while.
6actually.title: Are any of these questions actually "frequently asked"?

title.sub: Maybe I can already answer your question here.

update.1: Bumped version numbers for compatibility with 1.19 update.
update.2: Implemented text color shaders from Vanilla Tweaks, replacing the JSON language files.
update.3: Renamed pack files so you don't have to do anything else to play older game versions.

blizzy.description: For his custom songs available in the Sounds add-on. More tracks to come.
blizzy.name: Blizzy?

twitterFellas.description3: for your witty splash texts. You can find links to your respective tweets in
twitterFellas.name: Some fellas on Twitter

vanillatweaks.description: Fixes for text colors on a dark surface.
vanillatweaks.name: Vanilla Tweaks

do.headline: Do:
do.modifyOnlineContent: Use this pack, its add-ons or your modification in online content, like YouTube videos.
do.modifyPersonalUse: Use and modify Mintcraft and/or its add-ons to your liking and for personal use.
do.optionalCredit: Credit me somewhere when you use my pack by linking to this website.
do.serverPacks: Use this pack or parts of it for server resource packs. Remember to properly credit Vanilla Tweaks if you do.
do.sharePrivate: Share this pack, or your modified version of it, with friends privately.

dont.headline: Don't:
dont.redistribute: Host, mirror, sell or otherwise redistribute versions, parts and/or modifications of Mintcraft and/or its add-ons anywhere public for others to download.

addonFont.description: A font that I specifically made to use with Mintcraft. You can see it used in the screenshots further up on this page, as well as in these pack icons.
addonFont.name: Mintbit font add-on

mintcraft.description: My resource pack providing you with dark-mode containers and a selfish-color palette and many other tweaks. Some icons even move when you hover over them!
mintcraft.name: Mintcraft

saucecraft.description: Too lazy to download all packs individually? Download the latest version of Mintcraft and all add-ons combined in one handy pack.
saucecraft.name: The Full Sauce

steps.downloadPacks: Scroll down and click on a download button.
steps.moveToTop: Move my packs to the top of the list. If you want. I dunno what you like to use them with.
steps.placeInFolder: Place my packs in your resource pack folder. You can find yours by going to Options, Resource Packs and clicking on Open Pack Folder.

splashes.sub: All the splashes seen in Mintcraft.

title.sub: A Minecraft resource pack.

download.description: If you could actually read the text on slide 3 in a sensible order, you deserve a download button.
download.headline: MintSans V2

try.description: Click on the text below to enter your own.
try.headline: Try it out.
try.info: Currently, only basic Latin with a few special characters and digits is supported.
try.pangram: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.

title.sub: A sans-serif font.

mintalt.cardText: I looked at Google's Material icons, noticed some had text in them, and went "Huh." The good: Numbers. The bad: That stupid name. And the page for this font. That's still in the works.

mintcraft.cardText: What started as a simple "I don't like the colors of the default Minceraft UI" ended as a resource pack compatible with three game versions and two optional add-ons to change the font and button click sound.

mintsans.cardText: My first attempt at creating a geometric font family. A more optimized version is in development and will take at least thrice as long as it has taken me to make the following page.

startmenutiles.cardText: Branding is a pain. That's why everyone has their own that works best for them. With these 32 start menu tiles I made ages ago, I wanted to make them all feel at least somewhat more unified. And also fix Adobe's bullshit icons.

title.sub: Download and use some things I made.

creative.headline: Creative apps.
creative.subtitle: You enjoy pain? Great!
creative.text1: The Creative Cloud is a thing that... exists. For better or worse. It's still beyond me how a company that develops programs for designing stuff decided they should make all their apps' logos look like Media Encoder of all things.
creative.text2:
Anyway, you won't find that many icons in this pack, since I only made tiles for programs I actually use myself. Or have used, in some instances.
Unfortunately, the Creative Cloud is not one such instance.

download.headline: Start menu tiles
download.text1: For easily changing the tile image for any link in your start menu, I recommend
download.text2: . If you're on Windows 11, I'm sorry. You can grab something like
download.text3: to get the previous start menu back.

gaming.headline: Gaming.
gaming.subtitle: Waste your time.
gaming.text: I'm not asking where you got these ROMs from. I'm sure you acquired them totally legally and ripped yourself straight from the disc or cartridge.

social.headline: Socializing.
social.subtitle: Not everyone's strong suit.
social.text: This Discord tile is a perfect example for miscommunication between parts of my brain. That's at least the only logical explanation I can think of as to how this abomination came to be.

splashes.sub: All the splashes seen in Mintcraft.

title.sub: Provided you still have them.

downloads.worksPage: Looking for my other works? Click here to get to their new page.

anilist.text: Find out what my favorite shows are, and that I rewatched Nichijou 4 times.
anilist.title: AniList.

discord.text: Write with me and like-minded people and be the first to know about new announcements.
discord.title: Discord server.

github.text: Have a look at some source code, like for this website that won't run well on the PCs we use at work.
github.title: GitHub.

lastfm.text: Get confused about how I can listen to J-Pop right after German hip-hop and Ambient.
lastfm.title: Last.fm.

patreon.text: Join a level and support me financially, so I can afford licenses and assets.
patreon.title: Patreon.

about.text: Name's Nick. I'm an 18 year old German guy that like things with mint flavor and spending time with design, motion graphics or code. Or with books. Or anime.
about.title: About me.

youtube.text: All my animated works, with extra compression artifacts to stimulate your senses.
youtube.title: YouTube.

mintcraft16.fullText: This is a smaller update that bumps up the version to be compatible with the latest update of the game, as well as changes to how texts on dark surfaces are recolored (courtesy of Vanilla Tweaks). I also took some time to fix the file names for some packs and also added the respective game version at the end.
mintcraft16.headline: Mintcraft version 1.6 available.
mintcraft16.imageAlt: The Mintcraft logo with the version number 1.6 written left to it. In the background there's a stone block fading away downwards.

patreon.boldText: You can now support me financially as well.
patreon.fullText: The stress is on can. I won't keep entire works of mine behind a paywall. However, depending on which level you join, you will get a quick look at the things I'm working on, and will be able to give feedback as well. Some works I might alter to make them work as wallpapers, depending on how well that can be done.
patreon.headline: I opened up a Patreon.
patreon.imageAlt: The icons of the different levels, from left to right: A seed, a white flower bud, a flower with thin leaves and a yellow core, and an apple hanging from a branch with a couple of leaves.

twitterDelete.boldText: After about 7 years, I finally decided to give up this platform for good.
twitterDelete.fullText: A decision I made in favor of my mental health and privacy, but also to be less distracted while working, and to spend more time talking privately with my friends in a smaller circle. If you'd like to continue to keep up with me and the projects I'm working on, feel free to join my Discord server if you haven't already. After all, now I have an even bigger incentive to bring more life to the place.
twitterDelete.headline: Twitter account deleted.
twitterDelete.imageAlt: The Mintcraft logo with the version number 1.5 written left to it. In the background there's the improved recipe book texture fading away towards the left.

recent.description: Some still wonder why I write with capitalization and punctuation on Discord. One said I should write a book about my system. Click on the above image to not read it.
recent.name: A joke.
recent.title: Latest creation.

title.sub: I make things look neat with vector points, keyframes and colorful monospace letters.

contact.text: If you have any other privacy-related questions just send an email to privacy[at]pprmint.art.
contact.title: Any questions?

cookiesAcknowledged.text1: Used to check whether the cookie pop-up has been clicked away. If so, this key with the value

i18nextLng.text1: Used to check which language to use on the website, and is read on each visit. The value is either
i18nextLng.text3: for German. When you first visit this website, the preferred language is detected from your browser settings and written to the key accordingly. If you change the language using the website settings above, this value will be replaced.

text.long1: Currently, the following can be found in your browser's localStorage for this website:
text.long2: In the future, more keys might be added as more settings can be changed on the website. However, with these, you can't be uniquely identified or tracked, should that have been one of your worries now.
text.tldr: Settings such as your preferred language are, as the name suggests, stored locally in your browser's "localStorage" so you don't have to set them up again and again. Per definition, I guess this also counts as a "cookie".

externalLinks.text: Some pages may contain links to external websites. Any link that takes you to another website will have a top-right facing arrow and tends to be blue. Once you visit such external websites, you are subject to their respective privacy policies. I take no responsibility for the practices of nor content on said websites.
externalLinks.title: External links.

responsible.text: Responsible for content on this website is me, Nicklas Scharp. For privacy-related questions, feel free to send an email to privacy[at]pprmint.art.
responsible.title: Person responsible.

accessTime.description: The time when a client got a file from the server. If you care: The format's DD/MMMM/YYYY: HH:MM:SS +GMT.
accessTime.title: Access time.

agent.description: This thing tells the server infos like what browser, operating system and rendering engine is used. I can also tell you that Windows 11 is still referred to as "(Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64)" in there. For more info on what's in the User-Agent header, see:
agent.title: User-Agent.

domain.description: Imagine that: It's all pprmint.art. Who knew.
domain.title: Domain.

file.description: The path and name of the file that the client received, like the favicon, fonts and other assets.
file.title: Received file.

httpCodes.description: Usually that's "200 OK", because everything is awesome. If you wanna learn more about all the different codes that the HTTP world has to offer, see:
httpCodes.title: HTTP code.

ip.description: Nope, I got no clue where you live. The law requires my hosting provider to anonymize IP addresses, so the last octet of every IP address is replaced by a random value. I can confirm: The IP address that appears in the logs doesn't match my actual address, so that's cool.
ip.title: IP address.

storagePeriod.description: Each log entry is stored for up to 7 days.
storagePeriod.title: Storage period.

usage.description: Earlier I mentioned that statistics are created with this data. That's it. For a statistics fanboy like yours truly, this is of course a dream come true and most interesting to see that once 0.3% (tried to) access the site on Windows XP. Not that I think they'd be worth anything, but these logs aren't shared with anyone.
usage.title: My use.

text.tldr: Like most other web servers, this one collects some data about your browser when you visit this site and my hosting provider creates some nice statistics with this data. That's about it.

title.sub: Very exciting.
There's nothing to see here yet.
